channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Amnesty accuses Israel's government of 'ethnic cleansing' of West Bank Palestinians
item.description: The London-based rights group says the Israeli government is deliberately trying to annex the Palestinian territory.
item.content:encoded:
The London-based rights group says the Israeli government is deliberately trying to annex the Palestinian territory.

(Image credit: Mahmoud Illean)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Police blast water cannons at protesters amid unrest over stabbing in Belfast
item.description: Protesters in Northern Ireland set small fires and hurled bricks and bottles at the police during a second night of violence over a stabbing in Belfast. The incident sparked anti-immigrant violence.
item.content:encoded:
Protesters in Northern Ireland set small fires and hurled bricks and bottles at the police during a second night of violence over a stabbing in Belfast. The incident sparked anti-immigrant violence.

(Image credit: PA)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. launches a second day of strikes on Iran and Iran fires back at Gulf States
item.description: The new U.S. assault across multiple Iranian cities came as efforts to negotiate an end the war again appeared stuck, with Iran insisting it would maintain its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz
item.content:encoded:
The new U.S. assault across multiple Iranian cities came as efforts to negotiate an end the war again appeared stuck, with Iran insisting it would maintain its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: In the Ukraine war, new ground-based drones are playing a key role on the battlefield
item.description: Ukraine relies on robotic warfare to punch above its weight on the battlefield, including shifting frontline duties from soldiers to land drones.
item.content:encoded: Ukraine relies on robotic warfare to punch above its weight on the battlefield, including shifting frontline duties from soldiers to land drones.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: Somali World Cup referee denied U.S. entry, hailed as hero at home
item.description: A World Cup dream denied — Somali referee Omar Artan receives a hero's welcome at home after being blocked from entering the United States and taking part in the World Cup.
item.content:encoded:
A World Cup dream denied — Somali referee Omar Artan receives a hero's welcome at home after being blocked from entering the United States and taking part in the World Cup.

(Image credit: Abuukar Mohamed Muhidin)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Akinwotu

item.title: Taiwan drills with U.S. rocket system, firing in China's direction
item.description: The drill on Wednesday marked the first time the U.S.-supplied HIMARS system has been fired into the Taiwan Strait.
item.content:encoded:
The drill on Wednesday marked the first time the U.S.-supplied HIMARS system has been fired into the Taiwan Strait.

(Image credit: Chiang Ying-ying)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes. And, House passes ICE and Border Patrol funding
item.description: The U.S. and Iran have exchanged strikes after a helicopter was downed Monday near the Strait of Hormuz. And, House Republicans have passed a bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol through Trump's term.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Iran have exchanged strikes after a helicopter was downed Monday near the Strait of Hormuz. And, House Republicans have passed a bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol through Trump's term.

(Image credit: Ryan Murphy)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: China re‑centers North Korea ties as nuclear silence reshapes balance
item.description: Xi's silence on North Korea's nuclear program came as Kim tried to convince the world of the irreversibility of his country's status as a nuclear power.
item.content:encoded:
Xi's silence on North Korea's nuclear program came as Kim tried to convince the world of the irreversibility of his country's status as a nuclear power.

(Image credit: 朝鮮通信社)
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: China and North Korea renew ties, complicating U.S. response to Kim's nuclear program
item.description: With Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un reaffirming ties while sidestepping nuclear tensions, the U.S. faces a growing challenge in responding to an increasingly confident, nuclear-armed North Korea.
item.content:encoded: With Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un reaffirming ties while sidestepping nuclear tensions, the U.S. faces a growing challenge in responding to an increasingly confident, nuclear-armed North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: Israeli leader who pulled out of Lebanon warns against getting stuck again
item.description: Ehud Barak withdrew Israeli forces from Lebanon in 2000, ending an occupation that lasted nearly two decades. He says that was a quagmire Israel shouldn't repeat.
item.content:encoded:
Ehud Barak withdrew Israeli forces from Lebanon in 2000, ending an occupation that lasted nearly two decades. He says that was a quagmire Israel shouldn't repeat.

(Image credit: Alfi Ben Yaakov)
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre
